sections.paragraph:
οὐχί (a strengthened form of οὐ; Hom.+; Attic wr.; SIG 646, 41 [170 b.c.]; 834, 18; PSI 499, 4 [257/256 b.c.]; PEdg 111 [=Sb 6994], 27; LXX; En; TestSol, TestJob, JosAs; ApcEsdr 2:13=p. 26, 6 Tdf.; ApcMos; Jos., Ant. 17, 312 al.; Just., Tat.) ‘not’ (B-D-F §432; 427, 2; Rob. 296; 917 al.).

① a simple negative, not (so in many of the pass. mentioned above) οὐχὶ πάντες καθαροί ἐστε J 13:11; cp. vs. 10 (ἀλλʼ οὐχί as Jos., Ant. 8, 279; Just., A I, 6, 1); 14:22; 1 Cor 6:1. οὐχὶ μή Lk 18:30. οὐχὶ μᾶλλον not rather 1 Cor 5:2 (cp. Just., D. 95, 1f). Foll. by ἀλλά not … but (TestJob 38:6; Just., A I, 23, 1) 10:29; 2 Cor 10:13 v.l. (for οὐκ); 12:10.

② a negative reply, no, by no means w. ἀλλά foll. (X., Cyr. 1, 3, 4 codd.; Gen 18:15; 19:2; 42:12; TestAbr A 5 p. 82, 5 [Stone p. 12] al.; JosAs 1:14) Lk 1:60; 16:30; J 9:9; Ro 3:27. οὐχί, λέγω ὑμῖν, ἀλλά no, I tell you, but rather Lk 12:51; 13:3, 5. (S. Schwyzer II 597.)

③ an interrogative word in questions that expect an affirmative answer, not? (X., Cyr. 8, 3, 46; PGrenf I, 1 I, 25 [II b.c.]; Gen 40:8; Judg 4:6; TestAbr B 10 p. 115, 5 [Stone p, 78]; JosAs 6:5; ApcEsdr 2:13; Just., D. 49, 2) οὐχὶ καὶ οἱ τελῶναι τὸ αὐτὸ ποιοῦσιν; Mt 5:46. Cp. vs. 47; 6:25; 10:29; Lk 6:39; 12:6; 15:8; 17:17 (v.l. οὐχ); 24:26; J 11:9; Ro 3:29; 1 Cor 1:20; Hb 1:14; 1 Cl 31:2; D 1:3. ἀλλʼ οὐχὶ ἐρεῖ αὐτῷ … ; will he not rather say to him … ? Lk 17:8. ἢ οὐχὶ … ; 1 Cl 46:6.—διὰ τί οὐχὶ μᾶλλον ἀδικεῖσθε; 1 Cor 6:7a; cp. vs. 7b. πῶς οὐχὶ … ; (1 Esdr 4:32; Just., D. 18, 3; 89, 3) Ro 8:32.—DELG s.v. οὐ. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

  1. wr. = writers, writings

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

PEdg PEdg = CDE., Selected Papyri—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

foll. foll. = followed, following

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

Schwyzer Schwyzer = ES., Griechische Grammatik—List 6

PGrenf I PGrenf I = BGrenfell, An Alexandrian Erotic Fragment, etc.—List 4

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 742
